# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Create Docker Compose configuration for ELK stack (Elasticsearch, Logstash, Kibana). Define services for log collection, storage, and visualization. Configure Logstash pipelines for log parsing. Expose Kibana UI and Elasticsearch ports for access. This is for our centralized logging platform.
# Category: elasticsearch_logs_exposed

version: '3.8'

services:
  # Elasticsearch - Log Storage and Search Engine
  elasticsearch:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elasticsearch
    environment:
      - node.name=es-node-01
      - cluster.name=elk-cluster
      - discovery.type=single-node
      - bootstrap.memory_lock=true
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.security.http.ssl.enabled=false
      - xpack.security.transport.ssl.enabled=false
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - "ES_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms1g -Xmx1g"
      - xpack.license.self_generated.type=basic
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.threshold_enabled=true
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.low=85%
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.high=90%
      - cluster.routing.allocation.disk.watermark.flood_stage=95%
      - indices.lifecycle.poll_interval=1m
    ulimits:
      memlock:
        soft: -1
        hard: -1
      nofile:
        soft: 65536
        hard: 65536
    volumes:
      - elasticsearch_data:/usr/share/elasticsearch/data
      - ./elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml:ro
      - ./elasticsearch/config/ilm-policy.json:/usr/share/elasticsearch/config/ilm-policy.json:ro
    ports:
      - "9200:9200"
      - "9300:9300"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - >
          curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
          http://localhost:9200/_cluster/health | grep -q '"status":"green"\|"status":"yellow"'
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 60s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 2g
          cpus: "2.0"
        reservations:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: "0.5"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=elasticsearch"
      - "com.elk.description=Search and analytics engine"

  # Logstash - Log Collection and Parsing Pipeline
  logstash:
    image: docker.elastic.co/logstash/logstash:8.12.0
    container_name: logstash
    environment:
      - "LS_JAVA_OPTS=-Xms512m -Xmx512m"
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.enabled=true
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.username=elastic
      - xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.password=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - pipeline.workers=2
      - pipeline.batch.size=125
      - pipeline.batch.delay=50
      - config.reload.automatic=true
      - config.reload.interval=5s
      - log.level=info
      - queue.type=persisted
      - queue.max_bytes=1gb
    volumes:
      - ./logstash/config/logstash.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/logstash.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/config/pipelines.yml:/usr/share/logstash/config/pipelines.yml:ro
      - ./logstash/pipeline/:/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/:ro
      - ./logstash/patterns/:/usr/share/logstash/patterns/:ro
      - ./logstash/templates/:/usr/share/logstash/templates/:ro
      - logstash_data:/usr/share/logstash/data
      - logstash_dead_letter_queue:/usr/share/logstash/dead_letter_queue
    ports:
      # Beats input
      - "5044:5044"
      # TCP/JSON input
      - "5000:5000/tcp"
      - "5000:5000/udp"
      # Syslog input
      - "5514:5514/tcp"
      - "5514:5514/udp"
      # HTTP input for webhooks/API logs
      - "8080:8080"
      # Logstash monitoring API
      - "9600:9600"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - curl -s http://localhost:9600/_node/stats | grep -q '"status":"green"\|"status":"yellow"' || exit 1
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 90s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: "1.5"
        reservations:
          memory: 512m
          cpus: "0.25"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=logstash"
      - "com.elk.description=Log collection and parsing pipeline"

  # Kibana - Visualization and Dashboard UI
  kibana:
    image: docker.elastic.co/kibana/kibana:8.12.0
    container_name: kibana
    environment:
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - ELASTICSEARCH_USERNAME=kibana_system
      - ELASTICSEARCH_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTICSEARCH_SSL_VERIFICATIONMODE=none
      - xpack.security.enabled=true
      - xpack.encryptedSavedObjects.encryptionKey=${ENCRYPTION_KEY:-a]V4qN2xvz6B8y!LFAQ@Km7k#cMZuaE3}
      - xpack.reporting.encryptionKey=${REPORTING_KEY:-X3gR!jbz2xA@Kp8wLM5nFvQ9cYuDaE6t}
      - xpack.security.encryptionKey=${SECURITY_KEY:-z9B!nR3gV7xA@Kp8wLM5QcYuDaE6tFvj}
      - server.name=kibana
      - server.host=0.0.0.0
      - server.publicBaseUrl=http://localhost:5601
      - monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled=true
      - telemetry.enabled=false
      - newsfeed.enabled=false
      - logging.root.level=info
    volumes:
      - ./kibana/config/kibana.yml:/usr/share/kibana/config/kibana.yml:ro
      - kibana_data:/usr/share/kibana/data
    ports:
      - "5601:5601"
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - curl -s http://localhost:5601/api/status | grep -q '"level":"available"' || exit 1
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 10
      start_period: 120s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 1g
          cpus: "1.0"
        reservations:
          memory: 512m
          cpus: "0.25"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=kibana"
      - "com.elk.description=Visualization and dashboard UI"

  # Filebeat - Lightweight Log Shipper (for Docker container logs)
  filebeat:
    image: docker.elastic.co/beats/filebeat:8.12.0
    container_name: filebeat
    user: root
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
      - LOGSTASH_HOSTS=logstash:5044
    command: filebeat -e --strict.perms=false
    volumes:
      - ./filebeat/config/filebeat.yml:/usr/share/filebeat/filebeat.yml:ro
      - ./filebeat/modules.d/:/usr/share/filebeat/modules.d/:ro
      - filebeat_data:/usr/share/filebeat/data
      # Docker container logs
      - /var/lib/docker/containers:/var/lib/docker/containers:ro
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro
      # System logs
      - /var/log:/var/log/host:ro
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
      logstash:
        condition: service_healthy
    healthcheck:
      test:
        - CMD-SHELL
        - filebeat test output || exit 1
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 5
      start_period: 30s
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 256m
          cpus: "0.5"
        reservations:
          memory: 128m
          cpus: "0.1"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=filebeat"
      - "com.elk.description=Lightweight log shipper"

  # Curator - Index Lifecycle Management Helper
  curator:
    image: python:3.11-slim
    container_name: elk-curator
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - ELASTICSEARCH_HOSTS=http://elasticsearch:9200
    entrypoint: >
      /bin/sh -c '
        pip install elasticsearch-curator &&
        echo "Curator installed. Running cleanup schedule..." &&
        while true; do
          echo "[`date`] Running index cleanup..." &&
          curator_cli --host elasticsearch --port 9200 \
            --http_auth elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} \
            delete-indices --filter_list \
            "[{\"filtertype\":\"age\",\"source\":\"creation_date\",\"direction\":\"older\",\"unit\":\"days\",\"unit_count\":30}]" \
            2>/dev/null || true;
          echo "[`date`] Cleanup complete. Sleeping for 24 hours." &&
          sleep 86400;
        done
      '
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: unless-stopped
    deploy:
      resources:
        limits:
          memory: 128m
          cpus: "0.25"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=curator"
      - "com.elk.description=Index lifecycle management"

  # Setup service - initializes passwords and configurations
  setup:
    image: docker.elastic.co/elasticsearch/elasticsearch:8.12.0
    container_name: elk-setup
    environment:
      - ELASTIC_PASSWORD=${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme}
      - KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD=${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD:-changeme}
    command: >
      bash -c '
        echo "Waiting for Elasticsearch to be ready...";
        until curl -s -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} http://elasticsearch:9200 | grep -q "cluster_name"; do
          sleep 5;
        done;

        echo "Setting kibana_system password...";
        curl -s -X POST -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_security/user/kibana_system/_password \
          -d "{\"password\":\"${KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD:-changeme}\"}";

        echo "";
        echo "Creating ILM policy for log rotation...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_ilm/policy/logs-policy \
          -d "{
            \"policy\": {
              \"phases\": {
                \"hot\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"0ms\",
                  \"actions\": {
                    \"rollover\": {
                      \"max_primary_shard_size\": \"10gb\",
                      \"max_age\": \"1d\"
                    },
                    \"set_priority\": {
                      \"priority\": 100
                    }
                  }
                },
                \"warm\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"7d\",
                  \"actions\": {
                    \"forcemerge\": {
                      \"max_num_segments\": 1
                    },
                    \"shrink\": {
                      \"number_of_shards\": 1
                    },
                    \"set_priority\": {
                      \"priority\": 50
                    }
                  }
                },
                \"delete\": {
                  \"min_age\": \"30d\",
                  \"actions\": {
                    \"delete\": {}
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "Creating index template for application logs...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_index_template/app-logs-template \
          -d "{
            \"index_patterns\": [\"app-logs-*\"],
            \"template\": {
              \"settings\": {
                \"number_of_shards\": 1,
                \"number_of_replicas\": 0,
                \"index.lifecycle.name\": \"logs-policy\",
                \"index.lifecycle.rollover_alias\": \"app-logs\"
              },
              \"mappings\": {
                \"properties\": {
                  \"@timestamp\": {\"type\": \"date\"},
                  \"message\": {\"type\": \"text\"},
                  \"level\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"service\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"host\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"source\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"trace_id\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"span_id\": {\"type\": \"keyword\"},
                  \"http_status\": {\"type\": \"integer\"},
                  \"response_time_ms\": {\"type\": \"float\"},
                  \"client_ip\": {\"type\": \"ip\"},
                  \"geoip\": {
                    \"properties\": {
                      \"location\": {\"type\": \"geo_point\"}
                    }
                  }
                }
              }
            }
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "Creating index template for system logs...";
        curl -s -X PUT -u elastic:${ELASTIC_PASSWORD:-changeme} \
          -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
          http://elasticsearch:9200/_index_template/system-logs-template \
          -d "{
            \"index_patterns\": [\"system-logs-*\"],
            \"template\": {
              \"settings\": {
                \"number_of_shards\": 1,
                \"number_of_replicas\": 0,
                \"index.lifecycle.name\": \"logs-policy\"
              }
            }
          }";

        echo "";
        echo "=== ELK Stack Setup Complete ===";
        echo "Elasticsearch: http://localhost:9200";
        echo "Kibana:        http://localhost:5601";
        echo "Logstash API:  http://localhost:9600";
        echo "================================";
      '
    networks:
      - elk-network
    depends_on:
      elasticsearch:
        condition: service_healthy
    restart: "no"
    labels:
      - "com.elk.service=setup"
      - "com.elk.description=Initial setup and configuration"

networks:
  elk-network:
    driver: bridge
    ipam:
      config:
        - subnet: 172.28.0.0/16
    labels:
      - "com.elk.network=elk-stack"

volumes:
  elasticsearch_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      - "com.elk.volume=elasticsearch-data"
  logstash_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      - "com.elk.volume=logstash-data"
  logstash_dead_letter_queue:
    driver: local
    labels:
      - "com.elk.volume=logstash-dlq"
  kibana_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      - "com.elk.volume=kibana-data"
  filebeat_data:
    driver: local
    labels:
      - "com.elk.volume=filebeat-data"

# ============================================================
# Supporting Configuration Files (create these in your project)
# ============================================================

# --- FILE: .env ---
# ELASTIC_PASSWORD=your_secure_elastic_password
# KIBANA_SYSTEM_PASSWORD=your_secure_kibana_password
# ENCRYPTION_KEY=a_random_32_char_encryption_key!!
# REPORTING_KEY=a_random_32_char_reporting_key!!
# SECURITY_KEY=a_random_32_char_security_key!!
# COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME=elk-stack

# --- FILE: elasticsearch/config/elasticsearch.yml ---
# cluster.name: elk-cluster
# node.name: es-node-01
# network.host: 0.0.0.0
# http.port: 9200
# discovery.type: single-node

# --- FILE: kibana/config/kibana.yml ---
# server.name: kibana
# server.host: "0.0.0.0"
# elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# monitoring.ui.container.elasticsearch.enabled: true

# --- FILE: logstash/config/logstash.yml ---
# http.host: "0.0.0.0"
# xpack.monitoring.elasticsearch.hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
# path.config: /usr/share/logstash/pipeline
# config.reload.automatic: true
# config.reload.interval: 5s
# log.level: info
# dead_letter_queue.enable: true
# dead_letter_queue.max_bytes: 1024mb

# --- FILE: logstash/config/pipelines.yml ---
# - pipeline.id: beats-input
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/01-beats-input.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 2
#   pipeline.batch.size: 125
#
# - pipeline.id: syslog-input
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/02-syslog-input.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1
#
# - pipeline.id: tcp-json-input
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/03-tcp-json-input.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1
#
# - pipeline.id: http-input
#   path.config: "/usr/share/logstash/pipeline/04-http-input.conf"
#   pipeline.workers: 1

# --- FILE: logstash/pipeline/01-beats-input.conf ---
# input {
#   beats {
#     port => 5044
#     ssl => false
#     tags => ["beats"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   # Parse Docker container logs
#   if [container][name] {
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "service" => "%{[container][name]}" }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse JSON-formatted log messages
#   if [message] =~ /^\{/ {
#     json {
#       source => "message"
#       target => "parsed"
#       skip_on_invalid_json => true
#     }
#     if [parsed][level] {
#       mutate { add_field => { "level" => "%{[parsed][level]}" } }
#     }
#     if [parsed][trace_id] {
#       mutate { add_field => { "trace_id" => "%{[parsed][trace_id]}" } }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Parse common log formats
#   # Apache/Nginx access logs
#   if [fileset][name] == "access" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => [
#           "%{IPORHOST:client_ip} - %{DATA:user_name} \[%{HTTPDATE:access_time}\] \"%{WORD:http_method} %{DATA:url} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}\" %{NUMBER:http_status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes:int} \"%{DATA:referrer}\" \"%{DATA:user_agent}\" %{NUMBER:response_time_ms:float}",
#           "%{IPORHOST:client_ip} - %{DATA:user_name} \[%{HTTPDATE:access_time}\] \"%{WORD:http_method} %{DATA:url} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}\" %{NUMBER:http_status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes:int} \"%{DATA:referrer}\" \"%{DATA:user_agent}\""
#         ]
#       }
#       tag_on_failure => ["_grok_access_failure"]
#     }
#
#     # GeoIP lookup for client IPs
#     if [client_ip] {
#       geoip {
#         source => "client_ip"
#         target => "geoip"
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Parse user agent
#     if [user_agent] {
#       useragent {
#         source => "user_agent"
#         target => "ua"
#       }
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Application error logs
#   if [fileset][name] == "error" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => [
#           "\[%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:log_timestamp}\] \[%{LOGLEVEL:level}\] %{GREEDYDATA:error_message}",
#           "%{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:log_timestamp} %{LOGLEVEL:level} %{GREEDYDATA:error_message}"
#         ]
#       }
#       tag_on_failure => ["_grok_error_failure"]
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Normalize log levels
#   if [level] {
#     mutate {
#       uppercase => [ "level" ]
#     }
#   }
#
#   # Add processing metadata
#   mutate {
#     add_field => {
#       "pipeline" => "beats"
#       "[@metadata][index_prefix]" => "app-logs"
#     }
#     remove_field => ["agent", "ecs", "input", "log"]
#   }
#
#   # Date parsing
#   if [log_timestamp] {
#     date {
#       match => [ "log_timestamp", "ISO8601", "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss,SSS", "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.SSS" ]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#       remove_field => ["log_timestamp"]
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   elasticsearch {
#     hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#     user => "elastic"
#     password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#     index => "app-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#     manage_template => false
#   }
# }

# --- FILE: logstash/pipeline/02-syslog-input.conf ---
# input {
#   syslog {
#     port => 5514
#     type => "syslog"
#     tags => ["syslog"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if [type] == "syslog" {
#     grok {
#       match => {
#         "message" => [
#           "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{DATA:syslog_program}(?:\[%{POSINT:syslog_pid:int}\])?: %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}",
#           "%{SYSLOGTIMESTAMP:syslog_timestamp} %{SYSLOGHOST:syslog_hostname} %{GREEDYDATA:syslog_message}"
#         ]
#       }
#       tag_on_failure => ["_grok_syslog_failure"]
#     }
#
#     date {
#       match => [ "syslog_timestamp", "MMM  d HH:mm:ss", "MMM dd HH:mm:ss" ]
#       target => "@timestamp"
#     }
#
#     # Map syslog severity to standard log levels
#     if [severity] {
#       translate {
#         field => "severity"
#         destination => "level"
#         dictionary => {
#           "0" => "EMERGENCY"
#           "1" => "ALERT"
#           "2" => "CRITICAL"
#           "3" => "ERROR"
#           "4" => "WARNING"
#           "5" => "NOTICE"
#           "6" => "INFO"
#           "7" => "DEBUG"
#         }
#         fallback => "INFO"
#       }
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => {
#         "pipeline" => "syslog"
#         "service" => "%{syslog_program}"
#       }
#       remove_field => ["severity", "facility", "facility_label", "severity_label", "priority"]
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if [type] == "syslog" {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "system-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       manage_template => false
#     }
#   }
# }

# --- FILE: logstash/pipeline/03-tcp-json-input.conf ---
# input {
#   tcp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json_lines
#     type => "tcp-json"
#     tags => ["tcp", "json"]
#   }
#   udp {
#     port => 5000
#     codec => json_lines
#     type => "udp-json"
#     tags => ["udp", "json"]
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if [type] in ["tcp-json", "udp-json"] {
#     # Ensure timestamp is set
#     if [timestamp] {
#       date {
#         match => [ "timestamp", "ISO8601", "yyyy-MM-dd'T'HH:mm:ss.SSSZ", "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss" ]
#         target => "@timestamp"
#         remove_field => ["timestamp"]
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Normalize level field
#     if [level] {
#       mutate { uppercase => [ "level" ] }
#     } else if [severity] {
#       mutate {
#         rename => { "severity" => "level" }
#       }
#       mutate { uppercase => [ "level" ] }
#     } else {
#       mutate { add_field => { "level" => "INFO" } }
#     }
#
#     # Extract stack traces for error logs
#     if [level] in ["ERROR", "FATAL", "CRITICAL"] and [stack_trace] {
#       mutate {
#         add_tag => ["error", "has_stacktrace"]
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Fingerprint for deduplication
#     fingerprint {
#       source => ["message", "service", "level"]
#       target => "[@metadata][fingerprint]"
#       method => "SHA256"
#       concatenate_sources => true
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "pipeline" => "tcp-json" }
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if [type] in ["tcp-json", "udp-json"] {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "app-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       document_id => "%{[@metadata][fingerprint]}"
#       manage_template => false
#     }
#   }
# }

# --- FILE: logstash/pipeline/04-http-input.conf ---
# input {
#   http {
#     port => 8080
#     codec => json
#     type => "http"
#     tags => ["http", "webhook"]
#     response_headers => {
#       "Content-Type" => "application/json"
#       "Access-Control-Allow-Origin" => "*"
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# filter {
#   if [type] == "http" {
#     # Add source info from HTTP headers
#     if [headers] {
#       if [headers][x_forwarded_for] {
#         mutate { add_field => { "client_ip" => "%{[headers][x_forwarded_for]}" } }
#       }
#       mutate { remove_field => ["headers"] }
#     }
#
#     # Parse webhook payloads (GitHub, GitLab, etc.)
#     if "webhook" in [tags] {
#       if [repository] {
#         mutate {
#           add_field => { "service" => "webhook" }
#           add_tag => ["vcs-webhook"]
#         }
#       }
#     }
#
#     # Ensure timestamp
#     if ![timestamp] {
#       ruby {
#         code => "event.set('@timestamp', LogStash::Timestamp.now)"
#       }
#     }
#
#     mutate {
#       add_field => { "pipeline" => "http" }
#     }
#   }
# }
#
# output {
#   if [type] == "http" {
#     elasticsearch {
#       hosts => ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#       user => "elastic"
#       password => "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
#       index => "http-logs-%{+YYYY.MM.dd}"
#       manage_template => false
#     }
#   }
# }

# --- FILE: logstash/patterns/custom-patterns ---
# NGINX_ACCESS %{IPORHOST:client_ip} - %{DATA:user_name} \[%{HTTPDATE:access_time}\] "%{WORD:http_method} %{URIPATHPARAM:url} HTTP/%{NUMBER:http_version}" %{NUMBER:http_status:int} %{NUMBER:body_bytes:int} "%{DATA:referrer}" "%{DATA:user_agent}" %{NUMBER:request_time:float}
# SPRING_BOOT %{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:log_timestamp}\s+%{LOGLEVEL:level}\s+%{NUMBER:pid:int}\s+---\s+\[%{DATA:thread}\]\s+%{DATA:logger}\s+:\s+%{GREEDYDATA:message}
# PYTHON_LOG %{TIMESTAMP_ISO8601:log_timestamp} - %{DATA:logger} - %{LOGLEVEL:level} - %{GREEDYDATA:message}

# --- FILE: filebeat/config/filebeat.yml ---
# filebeat.inputs:
#   - type: container
#     paths:
#       - /var/lib/docker/containers/*/*.log
#     processors:
#       - add_docker_metadata:
#           host: "unix:///var/run/docker.sock"
#       - decode_json_fields:
#           fields: ["message"]
#           target: ""
#           overwrite_keys: true
#           add_error_key: true
#     multiline.pattern: '^\s+(at|\.{3})\s|^Caused by:|^\s+\.\.\.\s|^Exception'
#     multiline.negate: false
#     multiline.match: after
#
#   - type: log
#     enabled: true
#     paths:
#       - /var/log/host/syslog
#       - /var/log/host/auth.log
#     tags: ["system"]
#     fields:
#       source: system
#
# output.logstash:
#   hosts: ["logstash:5044"]
#   bulk_max_size: 2048
#   loadbalance: true
#
# processors:
#   - add_host_metadata: ~
#   - add_cloud_metadata: ~
#
# logging.level: info
# logging.to_files: false
#
# monitoring.enabled: true
# monitoring.elasticsearch:
#   hosts: ["http://elasticsearch:9200"]
#   username: elastic
#   password: "${ELASTIC_PASSWORD}"
